title: Build Phase 1: Core Customer Management and Scheduling

requirements.text: Create a customer database in the backend that stores customer records with fields for name, phone number, email address, physical address, and maintains a relationship to job history records

quotes: Customer database with full CRUD operations

acceptanceCriteria: Customer type defined in Motoko with all required fields
CRUD functions implemented for creating, reading, updating, and deleting customers
Stable storage used for customer persistence across upgrades
Each customer has a unique identifier

requirements.text: Implement frontend customer management interface with forms to add new customers, edit existing customer information, view customer details, and delete customer records

quotes: Customer database with full CRUD operations

acceptanceCriteria: Add customer form with validation for all fields
Customer list view showing all customers
Edit customer modal or page
Delete confirmation dialog
Success and error feedback for all operations

requirements.text: Create a job history data structure in the backend that stores job records linked to customers, including service date, service type, issues found, and job status

quotes: Job history timeline for each customer

acceptanceCriteria: Job type defined with fields for date, service type, issues, and status
Jobs are associated with customer IDs
Functions to add, retrieve, and update job records
Jobs are stored in stable storage

requirements.text: Implement a job history timeline component in the frontend that displays all past services for a selected customer in chronological order, showing service dates, types of work performed, and issues discovered

quotes: Job history timeline for each customer

acceptanceCriteria: Timeline component displays jobs in reverse chronological order
Each job entry shows date, service type, and issues found
Timeline is visible on customer detail page
Empty state shown when customer has no job history

requirements.text: Build a scheduling system in the backend that stores appointment records with customer ID, scheduled date and time, job type, and duration
acceptanceCriteria: Appointment type defined with date, time, customer reference, and job details
Functions to create, update, retrieve, and delete appointments
Appointments stored in stable storage
Ability to query appointments by date range

requirements.text: Create a calendar interface in the frontend that displays scheduled appointments in a monthly view with the ability to create new appointments, view appointment details, and navigate between months
acceptanceCriteria: Calendar displays current month with appointments
Clicking a date allows creating a new appointment
Appointments are visually displayed on their scheduled dates
Month navigation controls work correctly
Appointment detail modal shows full information

requirements.text: Implement a job status tracking system in the backend with three states (scheduled, in-progress, completed) and functions to update job status
acceptanceCriteria: Job status enum with exactly three states: scheduled, in-progress, completed
Status field included in job records
Function to update job status with validation
Ability to query jobs filtered by status

requirements.text: Create a job status management interface in the frontend that displays jobs grouped or filtered by status with the ability to update job status and view status-specific job lists
acceptanceCriteria: Job board or list view showing jobs by status
Visual distinction between scheduled, in-progress, and completed jobs
Ability to change job status with dropdown or status buttons
Filter or tab navigation to view jobs by specific status
Status change updates reflected immediately in the UI

requirements.text: Integrate Internet Identity authentication so that all customer data, job information, and business features are only accessible to authenticated users
acceptanceCriteria: Login flow using Internet Identity before accessing the app
Backend functions check caller identity and restrict access
Unauthenticated users are redirected to login
Logout functionality available in the UI
User identity displayed when logged in

requirements.text: Design and implement a mobile-responsive user interface with an earth tone color scheme using warm greens and browns, clear typography optimized for readability, intuitive navigation suitable for on-the-go business management, and consistent spacing and layout patterns across all pages

quotes: Mobile-friendly UI with earth tone color scheme (greens and browns)

acceptanceCriteria: Color palette uses earth tones (greens, browns) instead of blue or purple
All pages are fully responsive and usable on mobile devices
Typography is clear and legible on small screens
Navigation is accessible and intuitive on mobile
Consistent visual theme applied across all components and pages

constraints: All backend logic must remain in a single Motoko actor in backend/main.mo
Do not modify immutable frontend paths: useInternetIdentity hooks, useActor.ts, main.tsx, or components/ui files
Use only React, Tailwind CSS, Shadcn components, and React Query for the frontend
Avoid blue and purple color schemes; use earth tones (greens and browns) as specified
nonGoals: AI-powered social media post generator (deferred to Phase 3)
Template library for mold remediation content (deferred to Phase 3)
Content calendar for social media (deferred to Phase 3)
Post preview and editing system (deferred to Phase 3)
Drag-and-drop functionality for the calendar (basic calendar only in Phase 1)
